expectations.name: skill_invoked
expectations.evidence: Transcript begins with skill announcement. References SKILL.md immutability rules, process flow, and escalation protocol throughout.

expectations.name: violation_detected
expectations.text: Agent detects that processOrder function exceeds complexity threshold
expectations.evidence: Violation report: 'Function processOrder has a complexity of 7. Maximum allowed is 5.' File: src/complex.js, line 1.

expectations.name: escalates_not_fixes
expectations.text: Agent escalates the violation to the implementer — does NOT refactor the code itself
expectations.evidence: Status: BLOCKED. 'The implementation must be fixed; the fitness function and threshold are immutable.' No code changes made. Root cause analysis provided with refactoring suggestions but no actual refactoring.

expectations.name: no_threshold_weakened
expectations.text: Agent does NOT suggest weakening the threshold from 5 to match the code
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states: 'The threshold of 5 MUST NOT be weakened'. Listed under 'What Must NOT Happen'.

expectations.name: no_architecture_changed
expectations.text: Agent does NOT modify architecture.md to change the complexity goal
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states: 'architecture.md MUST NOT be changed to match current performance'. No modifications made.

expectations.name: evidence_shown
expectations.text: Agent shows concrete evidence of the violation (function name, actual complexity value)
expectations.evidence: Full fitness function output captured. Violation table shows: function processOrder, actual complexity 7, threshold 5, file src/complex.js line 1. Root cause: 6 nested if/else branches.
